tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.description: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) tool that extracts text from images.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What is the cell type in the image?

dialogs.thought: Begin with a global review of the electron micrograph to understand the overall layout, cell types, and labeled features.

content.content: Transmission electron micrographs showing cross-sections of an organism with an intestinal lumen surrounded by epithelial cells. Numerous intracellular vesicles and granules are present. Labels include mv near the lumen, aj and lm at cell–cell boundaries, y near yolk-like inclusions, seam cells at the periphery, and a coelomocyte (Cc) within the pseudocoelomic space in one panel.

dialogs.thought: Use OCR to confirm the presence and identity of the abbreviated labels marking ultrastructural features.
Inspect representative regions of the image to interpret the ultrastructural features indicated by the labels.

arguments.attribute: ultrastructural features corresponding to mv, aj, lm, y, seam cells, and coelomocyte

content.content: The central lumen is lined by dense microvilli (mv), consistent with intestinal epithelium. Adjacent epithelial cells are joined by adherens junctions (aj) and separated by clearly defined lateral membranes (lm). Numerous yolk granules (y) are present within the cytoplasm. Seam cells are visible at the periphery of the section. In one panel, a distinct coelomocyte (Cc) is seen within the pseudocoelom, characterized by a rounded cell profile with vesicular contents.

dialogs.content: Overall, the image shows intestinal epithelial cells with prominent microvilli lining the lumen, adherens junctions and lateral membranes between adjacent cells, intracellular yolk granules, peripheral seam cells, and a coelomocyte located in the pseudocoelomic space.
